name: Napa cabbage / Chinese cabbage, pe-tsai (food medium)

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: USDA FDC 169979 (total sugars 1.41 g) apportioned by Wang et al. 2021 Hortic Environ Biotechnol 10.1007/s13580-020-00294-y & Nawaz et al.
components.evidence: Total sugars 1.41 g/100g; Chinese-cabbage soluble sugar is dominated by glucose~fructose>>sucrose. Amount apportioned from USDA total sugars using published glucose:fructose:sucrose profile (~equal hexoses, minor sucrose)

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: USDA FDC 169979 total sugars apportioned; Frontiers fmolb 2022 10.3389/fmolb.2022.953189 (fructose~glucose main hexoses)
components.evidence: Fructose is co-dominant with glucose in internal head tissue; apportioned from USDA total sugars 1.41 g

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC 169979 total sugars apportioned; Springer 10.1007/s13580-020-00294-y
components.evidence: Sucrose is the minor sugar (glucose>fructose>>sucrose); apportioned from USDA total sugars

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Ferreres et al. J Food Nutr Res sciepub jfnr/2/11/5 (Brassica rapa greens 37-89 mg/100g); JAFC 6b01323 & PMC6915346 (malic dominant TCA acid in B. rapa pekinensis)
components.evidence: Malic acid is the dominant organic acid / TCA-cycle carbon source in Chinese cabbage; ~60 mg/100g representative (B. rapa species proxy, milder Napa head near mid-low range)

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Ferreres et al. sciepub jfnr/2/11/5 (B. rapa greens citric 56.75 mg/100g); Frontiers 10.3389/fmolb.2022.953189
components.evidence: Citric acid second most abundant organic acid; ~40 mg/100g representative for the milder head (species proxy)

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: MDPI Horticulturae 2023 9(12):1264 (oxalic acid measured, high CV 42%); classified low-oxalate leafy vegetable
components.evidence: Oxalic acid present but Chinese cabbage is a LOW-oxalate vegetable (unlike turnip greens); ~10 mg/100g representative, uncertain

components.name: fumaric acid
components.source: PMC6915346 (TCA intermediate detected in B. rapa pekinensis)
components.evidence: Minor TCA-cycle organic acid detected but not absolutely quantified

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: Minor TCA-cycle organic acid detected but not absolutely quantified

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: 0.288 g/100g (glutamic acid) - most abundant amino acid, key N source

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: 0.038 g/100g (L-serine; replaces implausible D-serine in current profile)

components.name: beta-carotene (provitamin A)
components.evidence: beta-carotene 190 ug/100g; RAE 16 ug. Provitamin A carotenoid (NOT retinol)

components.name: molybdate (Mo)
components.evidence: Trace micronutrient (nitrate reductase cofactor); present but not quantified in USDA panel

components.name: nickel (Ni)
components.evidence: Ultratrace plant micronutrient (urease cofactor); present, unquantified

components.name: sulfate
components.evidence: S source; Brassica accumulates sulfur (glucosinolates); present, unquantified

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: Compatible-solute osmolyte accumulated by Brassicas; present, unquantified

components.name: H2O / H+ / O2 / CO2
components.evidence: Retained standard aqueous/gas exchange metabolites for the growth medium (water, proton, oxygen, carbon dioxide)

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Animal vitamer of vitamin A; plants supply provitamin A as beta-/alpha-carotene, not preformed retinol. FooDB predicted/implausible for a plant matrix. Carotenes retained.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: Non-proteinogenic D-enantiomer; biologically implausible in plant protein. Replaced with L-serine (USDA).

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Compartment-tagged duplicate iron entry (BiGG/FooDB artifact); iron consolidated to a single elemental entry.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Redundant duplicate iron species; consolidated into one 'iron' entry (0.31 mg/100g).

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Cobalt is an ultratrace non-nutrient for a plant matrix (relevant only via B12, which plants do not synthesize); FooDB noise.

missing_added: malic acid (dominant organic acid - was absent)
fumaric acid / succinic acid (minor TCA acids)
riboflavin, pantothenic acid, vitamin E, vitamin K, folate quantified
keep_uncovered: quercetin (flavonol phytochemical; note FooDB formula C15H7O7 is wrong, correct is C15H10O7)
glucosinolates - gluconapin / glucobrassicin (Brassica-defining phytochemicals, no BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169979 (Cabbage, chinese pe-tsai, raw) - full nutrient panel: sugars, 18 amino acids, vitamins, minerals
Ferreres et al., J Food Nutr Res sciepub jfnr/2/11/5 (organic acids in Brassica rapa greens/tops - species proxy)
MDPI Horticulturae 2023, 9(12):1264 doi:10.3390/horticulturae9121264 (Chinese cabbage malic/citric/oxalic acid variation)
Wang et al. Hortic Environ Biotechnol doi:10.1007/s13580-020-00294-y (sugar accumulation, glucose>fructose>>sucrose)
Frontiers Mol Biosci doi:10.3389/fmolb.2022.953189 (B. rapa sugar/organic-acid metabolic diversity)
JAFC doi:10.1021/acs.jafc.6b01323 & PMC6915346 (pekinensis metabolite profiling: malic dominant TCA acid)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources are the free hexoses glucose + fructose (~1.2 g/100g combined, roughly equal, sucrose minor) plus malic acid as the leading organic acid; glutamate is the principal amino-acid nitrogen source. USDA total sugars (1.41 g) apportioned into specific sugars by published Chinese-cabbage hexose>>sucrose ratios (medium confidence). Organic-acid amounts are Brassica rapa species-level estimates (pekinensis-specific absolute quantification is sparse); Napa is a low-oxalate leafy vegetable. Removed animal retinol, D-serine, duplicate/compartment iron entries, and ultratrace cobalt as FooDB noise.
